Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible overtreatment of diabetes or poor nutritional status. Elevated creatinine indicates impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible nephropathy and/or urinary tract pathology. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status, review of diabetes management, and further evaluation for underlying renal disease.
